expectations.text: Launches more than one code-simplifier subagent since there are >5 changed files
expectations.evidence: Response ties code-simplifier scaling to the >5-file rule

expectations.text: Partitions files into non-overlapping groups (e.g., by module or directory) so each file appears in exactly one agent's scope
expectations.evidence: Response partitions changed files into non-overlapping groups

expectations.text: Also launches code-reviewer subagents (3 with distinct lenses)
expectations.evidence: Response also launches the required code-reviewer agents

expectations.text: Marks task T003 as [X] or completed because a clean TDD RED failure is a successful test-writing outcome
expectations.evidence: Response marks T003 complete or successful despite RED-state failures

expectations.text: Distinguishes between a clean RED failure (tests fail for the intended reason) and a broken test (syntax errors, import crashes)
expectations.evidence: Response distinguishes clean RED from broken tests

expectations.text: References TDD-first or RED-GREEN workflow as the reason this counts as success
expectations.evidence: Response references TDD/RED-GREEN workflow

expectations.text: Does NOT treat the test failures as an error that blocks progress
expectations.evidence: Response does not treat the expected RED state as a blocking error

expectations.text: Explicitly states that T010 and T011 cannot run in parallel because both touch src/search/service.ts
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly serializes T010/T011 because both touch src/search/service.ts

expectations.text: Schedules T012 in a wave that only includes non-overlapping work
expectations.evidence: Response places T012 into an explicit wave with non-overlapping work

expectations.text: Shows an explicit wave boundary or wait point before launching the next wave
expectations.evidence: Response shows multiple waves or an explicit wait point between them

expectations.text: Assigns work to specific subagents or wave slots instead of saying one phase-wide subagent will figure it out
expectations.evidence: Response assigns work by subagent/wave slot

expectations.text: Leaves T020 as [ ] or otherwise explicitly not complete because verification failed
expectations.evidence: Response keeps T020 incomplete after verification failure

expectations.text: Allows the already-running independent work for T022 to finish or be collected before deciding the next step
expectations.evidence: Response allows the already-running T022 work to finish or be collected

expectations.text: Marks T021 complete only if its own verification passed
expectations.evidence: Response marks T021 complete only after successful verification

expectations.text: States that the controller does not launch another wave until the failure is reported and resolved
expectations.evidence: Response blocks the next wave until failure handling completes

expectations.text: Includes all five progress reporting sections: Checklist Gate, Implementation Context Loaded, Phase Execution, Code Review Findings, Completion Validation
expectations.evidence: All five required progress sections are present

expectations.text: States that the controller owns the wave plan rather than delegating phase-wide scheduling to one subagent
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly assigns scheduling to the controller and describes wave/subagent planning

expectations.text: Launches separate implementation subagents in parallel for T003, T004, and T005 (or equivalent explicit same-wave dispatch)
expectations.evidence: Response groups T003/T004/T005 into an explicit parallel subagent dispatch

expectations.text: Keeps T006 sequential and keeps T007 after T003 because TDD or dependency ordering still applies
expectations.evidence: Response keeps T006 sequential and places T007 after T003/TDD dependency work

expectations.text: Defines a checkpoint decision format or equivalent explicit pass/fail criteria before advancing
expectations.evidence: Checkpoint Decision block includes explicit pass/fail status language

expectations.text: References TDD-first ordering where test tasks run before their corresponding implementation tasks
expectations.evidence: Response cites TDD/test-first ordering

expectations.text: Materializes review_scope_files from staged + unstaged + untracked files
expectations.evidence: Response materializes review_scope_files from staged, unstaged, and untracked files

expectations.text: States subagents must not recompute or narrow scope independently
expectations.evidence: Response states that review subagents must not narrow scope

expectations.text: Reports missing files when only a subset is reviewed
expectations.evidence: Response reports omitted files as missing

expectations.text: Applies Completion Gate with INCOMPLETE status when Missing Files > 0
expectations.evidence: Response applies an INCOMPLETE completion gate
